top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 124, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
12,513ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Nove Colli's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
101ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Nove Colli. Train it until it feels boring.
Granfondo Nove Colli's romagna Apennine foothills forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 124 miles.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 124 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 12,513ft of climbing on romagna Apennine foothills does to your power file on race day.
At Granfondo Nove Colli, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
124 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on romagna Apennine foothills. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 124 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Granfondo Nove Colli’s 124 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Granfondo Nove Colli. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, VO2max, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 124 miles of romagna apennine foothills in Cesenatico, Emilia-Romagna, Italy.
